texto: What I felt was, if you spend your life just writing fiction, you are going to falsify your material. And the fictional form was going to force you to do things with the material, to dramatize it in a certain way. I thought nonfiction gave one a chance to explore the world, the other world, the world that one didn't know fully.
explicacao: Citação de V. S. Naipaul sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “What I felt was, if you spend your life just writing fiction, you are going to falsify your mater…” articula vida, writing, world em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como termino e writing.

frase_original: What I felt was, if you spend your life just writing fiction, you are going to falsify your material. And the fictional form was going to force you to do things with the material, to dramatize it in a certain way. I thought nonfiction gave one a chance to explore the world, the other world, the world that one didn't know fully.

texto: A businessman is someone who buys at ten and is happy to get out at twelve. The other kind of man buys at ten, sees it rise to eighteen and does nothing. He is waiting for it to get to twenty. The beauty of numbers. When it drops to ten again he waits for it to get back to eighteen. When it drops to two he waits for it to get back to ten. Well, it gets back there. But he has wasted a quarter of his life. And all he's got out of his money is a little mathematical excitement.
explicacao: Citação de V.S. Naipaul sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “A businessman is someone who buys at ten and is happy to get out at twelve. The other kind of man…” articula negocios, business, practical em registro de negocios, com relevância para contextos como practical.

texto: What matters in the end in literature, what is always there, is the truly good. And -- though played out forms can throw up miraculous sports like The Importance of Being Earnest or Decline and Fall-- what is good is always what is new, in both form and content. What is good forgets whatever models it might have had, and is unexpected; we have to catch it on the wing. ((p. 62, Reading & Writing)
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a V.S. Naipaul: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. And -- though pla…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, literature, what-s-good pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e what-s-good.
